message:
fcp-core + fcp-host: honest instance-binding in host verifier (br-5qp7o)

The fcp-host gateway's `verify_live_request` constructed the
`CapabilityVerifier` with `InstanceId::new()` — a fresh random UUID
per request. The verifier's instance-binding check said:

    if let Some(inst_val) = claims.get(INSTANCE_ID) {
        if inst_str != self.instance_id.as_str() {
            return Err(ZoneViolation);
        }
    }
    // else: no instance check at all

With a per-request random verifier id, this was worst-of-both-worlds:
  (a) Every token that DID declare `instance_id` was rejected — the
      caller's claim can never match a UUID the host just invented.
      Instance-bound tokens were architecturally banned at the host.
  (b) Every token that did NOT declare `instance_id` passed without
      any check. Binding was silently opt-in for the token issuer and
      un-enforced by the gateway.

Root cause: the gateway has no link from a capability token back to
the specific SubprocessConnector that will execute the operation —
handshake uses `requested_instance_id: None` and the connector
chooses its own id, which the gateway never captures.

Fix (bead's recommended "honest" option 1):

- `CapabilityVerifier.instance_id` becomes `Option<InstanceId>`. The
  existing `new(key, zone, id)` constructor wraps `Some(id)` so every
  existing call site (50+ test files, connector runtime, fwc/bench)
  keeps its semantics unchanged.
- New `CapabilityVerifier::without_instance_binding(key, zone)`
  constructs a verifier that declines the binding check. When the
  token declares an `instance_id` claim, the match check is SKIPPED —
  the verifier is declaring "I can't enforce this, defer to
  downstream." Parser-level rejection of non-Text INSTANCE_ID still
  fires in both modes (malformed is malformed regardless of match).
- `fcp-host::verify_live_request` switches to
  `without_instance_binding`. Net: instance-bound tokens now reach
  the connector rather than being rejected at the gateway, where
  the check is meaningful because the connector KNOWS its own id.

Tests (in fcp-core/src/capability.rs):
- without_instance_binding_accepts_token_that_declares_instance_id:
  the motivating regression — a legitimate instance-bound token must
  now reach the connector, not bounce at the gateway.
- without_instance_binding_still_rejects_non_text_instance_id:
  parser-level defense preserved in the unbound mode.
- without_instance_binding_ignores_tokens_without_instance_claim:
  behavior parity with bound-mode for the common unbound-token case.

Split commit: t623k work (X-Principal header wiring on invoke_handler)
is actively in flight by a parallel agent in the same file; I staged
only my 5qp7o hunks (import removal + verifier construction) via a
filtered patch so their unstaged work is preserved in the worktree.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
